016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor;
018
019import java.lang.reflect.*;
020import java.util.*;
021import java.util.function.*;
022import java.util.logging.*;
023
024/**
025 * Bridges SLF4J MDC context from the request (dispatch) thread to the {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture}
026 * completion thread.
027 *
028 * <h5 class='topic'>Problem</h5>
029 * <p>
030 * When a {@code @RestOp} method returns a {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture}, the {@link AsyncResponseProcessor}
031 * registers a {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletionStage#whenComplete(BiConsumer) whenComplete} callback that runs on
032 * whatever thread completes the future &mdash; typically a different thread from the one that processed the request.
033 * SLF4J's {@code MDC} is backed by a {@link ThreadLocal}, so any diagnostic context set by an upstream filter
034 * (e.g. {@code requestId}, {@code userId}) is invisible to log statements emitted inside that callback.
035 *
036 * <h5 class='topic'>Solution</h5>
037 * <p>
038 * {@code MdcAsyncListener} provides a single factory method, {@link #wrap(BiConsumer, Map)}, that wraps a
039 * {@code whenComplete} callback with the standard MDC snapshot-restore-clear pattern:
040 * <ol>
041 *   <li>Snapshot the request thread's MDC map via {@code MDC.getCopyOfContextMap()} at dispatch time.
042 *   <li>On entry to the completion callback: save the completion thread's current MDC (if any) and install
043 *       the request-thread snapshot via {@code MDC.setContextMap(snapshot)}.
044 *   <li>Callback body runs with the request-thread MDC visible.
045 *   <li>In the {@code finally} block: restore the completion thread's original MDC state (if it had one) or
046 *       call {@code MDC.clear()} &mdash; this removes exactly the keys the listener installed without disturbing
047 *       any MDC state the completion thread itself had before the callback.
048 * </ol>
049 *
050 * <h5 class='topic'>Lazy skip</h5>
051 * <p>
052 * {@link #snapshot()} returns {@code null} when the request thread's MDC is empty or when SLF4J is absent.
053 * {@link #wrap(BiConsumer, Map)} returns the original callback unchanged when the snapshot is {@code null}, so no
054 * work is done on the completion thread for the common case of no MDC state.
055 *
056 * <h5 class='topic'>SLF4J detection</h5>
057 * <p>
058 * {@code MdcAsyncListener} uses {@link Class#forName(String)} to probe for {@code org.slf4j.MDC} at class-load
059 * time. SLF4J is NOT a compile-time dependency of {@code juneau-rest-server}. If SLF4J is absent from the
060 * runtime classpath, the class gracefully degrades: {@link #isAvailable()} returns {@code false},
061 * {@link #snapshot()} returns {@code null}, and {@link #wrap(BiConsumer, Map)} returns the original callback.
062 *
063 * <h5 class='topic'>Limitation</h5>
064 * <p>
065 * This class bridges SLF4J MDC only. Users of Log4j2's {@code ThreadContext} directly (without the SLF4J facade)
066 * will not benefit from this listener. That is documented as a v1 limitation; the SLF4J MDC is the lingua franca
067 * for structured logging context propagation in the JVM ecosystem.
068 *
069 * @see AsyncResponseProcessor
070 * @see org.apache.juneau.rest.server.RestContext#isMdcAsyncPropagation()
071 * @since 10.0.0
072 */
073public final class MdcAsyncListener {
074
075   private static final Logger LOG = Logger.getLogger(MdcAsyncListener.class.getName());
076
077   /** {@code true} if {@code org.slf4j.MDC} was found on the classpath at class-load time. */
078   private static final boolean AVAILABLE;
079
080   private static final Method GET_COPY_OF_CONTEXT_MAP;
081   private static final Method SET_CONTEXT_MAP;
082   private static final Method CLEAR;
083
084   /** MDC keys carrying the active trace / span id &mdash; the convention used by the OTel logback / log4j appenders. */
085   static final String MDC_TRACE_ID = "trace_id";
086   static final String MDC_SPAN_ID = "span_id";
087
088   /** {@code true} if {@code io.opentelemetry.api.trace.Span} was found on the classpath at class-load time. */
089   private static final boolean OTEL_AVAILABLE;
090
091   private static final Method SPAN_CURRENT;
092   private static final Method SPAN_GET_SPAN_CONTEXT;
093   private static final Method CTX_IS_VALID;
094   private static final Method CTX_GET_TRACE_ID;
095   private static final Method CTX_GET_SPAN_ID;
096
097   static {
098      boolean available = false;
099      Method copyOf = null;
100      Method set = null;
101      Method clr = null;
102      try {
103         Class<?> mdc = Class.forName("org.slf4j.MDC");
104         copyOf = mdc.getMethod("getCopyOfContextMap");
105         set = mdc.getMethod("setContextMap", Map.class);
106         clr = mdc.getMethod("clear");
107         available = true;
108      } catch (Exception e) {
109         LOG.fine(() -> "SLF4J MDC not found on classpath — MdcAsyncListener is a no-op: " + e.getMessage());
110      }
111      AVAILABLE = available;
112      GET_COPY_OF_CONTEXT_MAP = copyOf;
113      SET_CONTEXT_MAP = set;
114      CLEAR = clr;
115
116      boolean otel = false;
117      Method spanCurrent = null;
118      Method getSpanContext = null;
119      Method isValid = null;
120      Method getTraceId = null;
121      Method getSpanId = null;
122      try {
123         Class<?> span = Class.forName("io.opentelemetry.api.trace.Span");
124         Class<?> spanContext = Class.forName("io.opentelemetry.api.trace.SpanContext");
125         spanCurrent = span.getMethod("current");
126         getSpanContext = span.getMethod("getSpanContext");
127         isValid = spanContext.getMethod("isValid");
128         getTraceId = spanContext.getMethod("getTraceId");
129         getSpanId = spanContext.getMethod("getSpanId");
130         otel = true;
131      } catch (Exception e) {
132         LOG.fine(() -> "OpenTelemetry API not found on classpath — trace/span id is not propagated across async boundaries: " + e.getMessage());
133      }
134      OTEL_AVAILABLE = otel;
135      SPAN_CURRENT = spanCurrent;
136      SPAN_GET_SPAN_CONTEXT = getSpanContext;
137      CTX_IS_VALID = isValid;
138      CTX_GET_TRACE_ID = getTraceId;
139      CTX_GET_SPAN_ID = getSpanId;
140   }
141
142   private MdcAsyncListener() {}
143
144   /**
145    * Returns whether SLF4J MDC is available on the runtime classpath.
146    *
147    * <p>
148    * When {@code false}, all methods on this class are no-ops.
149    *
150    * @return {@code true} if {@code org.slf4j.MDC} was found at class-load time.
151    */
152   public static boolean isAvailable() {
153      return AVAILABLE;
154   }
155
156   /**
157    * Takes a snapshot of the current thread's SLF4J MDC map.
158    *
159    * <p>
160    * Call this on the request/dispatch thread before submitting async work. The returned map is passed to
161    * {@link #wrap(BiConsumer, Map)} to decorate the completion callback.
162    *
163    * <p>
164    * Returns {@code null} when:
165    * <ul>
166    *   <li>SLF4J is absent from the classpath, or
167    *   <li>The current thread's MDC is {@code null} or empty (lazy-skip contract — no work is done on the
168    *       completion thread when the request had no MDC context).
169    * </ul>
170    *
171    * @return A copy of the current MDC map, or {@code null}.
172    */
173   @SuppressWarnings({
174      "unchecked", // Type erasure on reflective/generic cast; element type is verified at call site
175      "java:S1168" // null is a meaningful lazy-skip sentinel: wrap() treats null as "no MDC to propagate"; an empty map would force unnecessary MDC installation.
176   })
177   public static Map<String, String> snapshot() {
178      if (!AVAILABLE)
179         return null;
180      try {
181         Map<String, String> map = (Map<String, String>) GET_COPY_OF_CONTEXT_MAP.invoke(null);
182         // Enrich the snapshot with the active OTel trace/span id (under the conventional OTel
183         // appender keys) so log correlation survives the async-completion hop even when the
184         // completion thread has lost the OTel context. Only when an OTel span is actually active.
185         map = enrichWithTraceContext(map);
186         return (map == null || map.isEmpty()) ? null : map;
187      } catch (Exception e) {
188         LOG.fine(() -> "MDC.getCopyOfContextMap() failed: " + e.getMessage());
189         return null;
190      }
191   }
192
193   @SuppressWarnings({
194      "java:S3011", // Reflective access to the OTel trace API — intentional; OpenTelemetry is not a compile dep.
195      "java:S1168"  // null preserves the lazy-skip contract: a null map with no active trace must stay null (no MDC to propagate).
196   })
197   private static Map<String, String> enrichWithTraceContext(Map<String, String> map) {
198      if (!OTEL_AVAILABLE)
199         return map;
200      try {
201         Object span = SPAN_CURRENT.invoke(null);
202         if (span == null)
203            return map;
204         Object ctx = SPAN_GET_SPAN_CONTEXT.invoke(span);
205         if (ctx == null || ! ((Boolean) CTX_IS_VALID.invoke(ctx)).booleanValue())
206            return map;
207         var out = (map == null) ? new HashMap<String, String>(4) : new HashMap<>(map);
208         out.put(MDC_TRACE_ID, String.valueOf(CTX_GET_TRACE_ID.invoke(ctx)));
209         out.put(MDC_SPAN_ID, String.valueOf(CTX_GET_SPAN_ID.invoke(ctx)));
210         return out;
211      } catch (Exception e) {
212         LOG.fine(() -> "OTel trace-context enrichment failed: " + e.getMessage());
213         return map;
214      }
215   }
216
217   /**
218    * Wraps a {@link BiConsumer} (typically a {@link java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture#whenComplete
219    * whenComplete} callback) so the completion thread sees the request thread's MDC context.
220    *
221    * <p>
222    * The wrapping logic:
223    * <ol>
224    *   <li>Save the completion thread's current MDC map (usually {@code null} for pool / virtual threads).
225    *   <li>Install {@code requestMdc} on the completion thread via {@code MDC.setContextMap(requestMdc)}.
226    *   <li>Invoke {@code action} inside a {@code try} block.
227    *   <li>{@code finally}: if the completion thread had its own MDC, restore it; otherwise call
228    *       {@code MDC.clear()} — either way only the keys the listener installed are removed.
229    * </ol>
230    *
231    * <p>
232    * If {@code requestMdc} is {@code null} (e.g. request thread had no MDC, or SLF4J absent), the original
233    * {@code action} is returned unchanged — zero overhead on the completion thread.
234    *
235    * @param <T>        The future result type.
236    * @param action     The callback to wrap.
237    * @param requestMdc The MDC snapshot taken on the dispatch thread (from {@link #snapshot()}), or {@code null}.
238    * @return A wrapped callback, or {@code action} itself when wrapping is not needed.
239    */
240   public static <T> BiConsumer<T, Throwable> wrap(BiConsumer<T, Throwable> action, Map<String, String> requestMdc) {
241      if (!AVAILABLE || requestMdc == null)
242         return action;
243
244      return (value, error) -> {
245         // Save the completion thread's pre-existing MDC (so we can restore it, not wipe it).
246         var before = snapshot();
247         try {
248            setContextMap(requestMdc);
249            action.accept(value, error);
250         } finally {
251            if (before != null)
252               setContextMap(before);
253            else
254               mdcClear();
255         }
256      };
257   }
258
259   @SuppressWarnings({
260      "java:S3011" // Reflective access to MDC methods — intentional; SLF4J is not a compile dep.
261   })
262   static void setContextMap(Map<String, String> map) {
263      try {
264         SET_CONTEXT_MAP.invoke(null, map);
265      } catch (Exception e) {
266         LOG.fine(() -> "MDC.setContextMap() failed: " + e.getMessage());
267      }
268   }
269
270   @SuppressWarnings({
271      "java:S3011" // Reflective access to MDC methods — intentional.
272   })
273   static void mdcClear() {
274      try {
275         CLEAR.invoke(null);
276      } catch (Exception e) {
277         LOG.fine(() -> "MDC.clear() failed: " + e.getMessage());
278      }
279   }
280}
